change: WS-15 + 4ep cosine decay (curves/points 64), d=32 [1,4,3,3,3]
note: HP-tuned + decay; beats d=128 baseline on both; deployed (int4 both-low-rank) targets met

change: num_curves/num_points 64->128
note: +16.5k params for ZERO gain (ahead worse, imm tied); head resolution is not the lever

change: channel_mixer_factor 1.0->1.5
note: +14k FFN-capacity HURTS both -> d=32 model is DATA-limited at 100u, not capacity-limited

change: WS-15 + 8-epoch decay (vs 4ep)
note: longer decay: ahead slightly worse, imm +0.00013 (< gate); 4-epoch decay is fine

change: WS-18 + 4ep decay (vs WS-15)
note: epochs SATURATE at 15 on 100 users (ahead worse, imm better) -> motivates the more-DATA experiment

change: 1 epoch WS on 1500 users (1000-2499), WS-only (no decay)
note: DATA VARIETY beats repetition: beats the WS-15+4ep-decay champion on BOTH modes, WS-ONLY. Decay phase next. Confirms data-limited -> supports the 5k direction

change: t1500ws + 0.27-epoch cosine decay (1500 users; WS:decay ratio = champion's 15:4)
note: data-variety recipe (1 epoch on ~1500 users + decay) BEATS the 100u/15ep champion by +0.0051 ahead / +0.0038 imm -- the +0.0038 imm exceeds the entire iter0->old-champion loop. fp32; deployed (quant+low-rank) number pending. Confirms data-limited -> scale toward 5k (needs the smaller/faster model)

change: H=2/K=16 (n_heads=2, head_dim=16) via the new K<32 CUDA kernel; 1500u champion recipe (1ep WS 1000-2499 + 0.27ep decay)
note: SIZE/SPEED win (efficiency-budget gate, NOT the +0.0003 monotonic gate). Per-card WKV state HALVED 1088->576 floats; WS train 1.16x faster (1.182 vs 1.020 steps/s); accuracy PARITY (both modes within 0.0002, far inside +0.0015 budget). Still beats d=128 baseline +0.0106 ahead/+0.0053 imm. K<32 kernel parity-verified (test_k16_wkv.py). Next: re-tune HPs (smaller model + larger data). Rust deployed rev/s pending K<32 engine port. ckpt scratchpad/exp_h2k16/h2k16d_904.pth -> reference/champ_h2k16.safetensors

change: FIRST 5k champion run: train 1-5000 (2ep WS = 13108 steps + 0.5ep decay = 3277), quant-aware q72u with per-run LEARNABLE codebooks (cb exports at both seams), champion HPs (peak_lr 1e-3, warmup 200, wd 0.01, clip 0.25), MAX=110000, eval 5001-10000
note: PROMOTED to champion_5k.json (ckpt champ5kd_3277.pth + its learned cbs; WS step trace = the Wilcoxon prune ref). vs d=128 fp target on the same 5000 users: -0.010187 ahead / -0.013418 imm (paired one-sided Wilcoxon p=1.0 both -- behind the target, as expected for the 15x-smaller quant-aware model; the phase's job is closing this). Wall-clock: WS 5h00m (~1.36 s/step avg), decay 72 min, eval 2x-sharded 66 min (+14 min resume) -> ~7.0h clean pipeline. Two latent bugs hit+fixed: LEARN=1 optim resume param-group mismatch (f71f43b), per-user lmdb env leak in get_benchmark_info killed eval shard 0 at user 2007 (7d095e3; n=5000 finish gate caught it). Tuner baseline (5001-5200 subset): ahead 0.294204 imm 0.270881. Next: hp_tuner_5k loop.

change: BUDGET A/B: champion recipe at HALF budget -- WS 1 ep (6554 steps) + 0.25 ep decay (1638), everything else identical to champ5k_r1 (champion HPs, q72u learnable cbs)
note: vs champ5k_r1 paired on the same 5000 users: ahead -0.000058 (p=0.31, indistinguishable), imm +0.000430 BETTER (p=6.1e-62). The 2nd WS epoch (same 5000 users reshuffled) adds NOTHING -- consistent with the data-variety-beats-repetition lesson. ADOPTED (Andrew's rule): WS 1 ep + ratio-0.25 decay is now the budget for ALL 5k runs (tuner trials AND research runs); champion runs drop ~7h -> ~3.5h. SIZE/SPEED-class accept (efficiency budget, p-gate exempt; imm improvement is a bonus). Promoted: ckpt champ5kb1d_1638.pth + its learned cbs; 6554-step WS trace = the new prune ref. Wall-clock: WS 2h27m, decay 37m, eval 89m (2-sharded).

change: HP-TUNED config from hp_tuner_5k coordinate descent (20 trials incl. re-probes, tune-eval 5001-5200): weight_decay 0.01->0.2 + dropout_scale 1.0->0.5, all other coords settled AT champion values (peak_lr 1e-3, warmup 200, clip 0.25, decay_ratio 0.25, adamw_beta2 0.999, cb_lr_mult 1.0); standard 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs
status: REJECTED (worse both modes at full eval)
note: SUBSET-OVERFIT LESSON: the tuned config WON the 200-user tune-eval by +0.000762 ahead / +0.000978 imm vs the tuner baseline, with real-looking paired signal inside the subset (wd 0.1->0.2 imm p=5e-8 on those 200 users) -- and still INVERTED at n=5000 (paired vs champ5k_b1: ahead -0.000545, imm -0.000677, p=1.0 both). Per-user pairing within a subset cannot see subset-vs-population mismatch; 200 users cannot resolve sub-0.001 HP effects. CONCLUSIONS: (1) champ5k_b1 REMAINS champion, its HPs (wd 0.01, dropout 1.0) are confirmed vs 19 alternatives spanning peak_lr/warmup/wd/clip/decay_ratio/beta2/dropout/cb_lr; (2) any future sub-0.001 tuner verdict needs full-eval confirmation before adoption; (3) HP tuning is CLOSED for this phase -- next lever = state-size ladders (deck/preset/global). Descent detail in tuner_5k_log.jsonl; clean grid landscapes: wd peaked mid-grid on the subset (0.05 worst, 0.2 best), beta2 monotone to 0.999, dropout extremes (0, 2x) clearly bad, cb_lr_mult 0.1/10 both bad. Wall-clock ~4.6h (WS 2h29m, decay 37m, sharded eval 92m). Validation prune ran clean all tuning: 1 crash-bug fixed (Tensor sidecar), 0 kills fired (joint-AND held vs single-mode transients, incl. cb_lr_mult=10 imm-only breach) -- honest evals throughout.

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER deck rung 1: deck stream n_heads 2->1 at fixed d_model=32 (RWKV_STREAM_HEADS=deck:1) -- deck per-entity WKV state 2304->4352 floats (1.89x) at 264 FEWER params (per-head k/v_scale_linear heads shrink); card/note state unchanged; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs
note: Doubling the deck stream's recurrent memory for free (params actually -264) does NOT help: paired vs champ5k_b1 ahead -0.000271 / imm -0.000238, p=1.0 both -- within ~1 sigma of zero, so read as NO EFFECT rather than harm. The deck stream is not state-capacity-limited at d=32/H=2, and the 2x-bigger single-head WKV memory buys nothing (weak evidence that 2 independent heads >= 1 double-size head at equal d_model). Val trajectory tracked the champion within noise the whole WS (deltas -0.0021..+0.0031, no prune strikes). Deck knob of the ladder CLOSED (rung 1 gained <0.0003); per Andrew's order next knob = preset, but the same H-lever mechanism just failed on deck, so the better-motivated free rung is user/global H=1 (long-recurrence streams are the state-sensitive ones per the blanket-quant lesson) -- preset first per the specified order. Deploy note: rejected change would have grown per-deck deploy state 9->17 KB fp32 with zero accuracy return. Wall-clock 4.6h (WS 2h25m @ 1.43s/step -- deck K=32 costs ~18% step time, decay 36m, sharded eval 91m). Infra banked: RWKV_STREAM_HEADS / RWKV_STREAM_LAYERS per-stream arch hooks (commit d6fca68).

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER preset rung 1 (iter 5): preset stream n_heads 2->1 at fixed d_model=32 (RWKV_STREAM_HEADS=preset:1) -- preset per-entity WKV state 1728->3264 floats (1.89x, cap 10x) at 198 fewer params; card/note/deck unchanged; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs
note: Same verdict as the deck rung (iter 4): paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2) ahead -0.000215 / imm -0.000445, p=1.0 both -- no effect to slightly worse. The stronger prior (preset is long-recurrence: ~whole-history sequences, the state-sensitive class per the blanket-quant lesson) did NOT materialize: doubling the preset stream's recurrent memory buys nothing. H=1 free-state mechanism now 0/2 across stream types; last knob = user/global H=1 (iter 6), then the ladder closes and the >=50-iteration research phase begins (layer-adds move to the research phase as capacity questions -- no H-rung showed a state signal). OPS INCIDENT: the 2-parallel-shard eval WEDGED (both shards 50-85+ min on mega-users, 11.5/12 GB VRAM = WDDM oversubscription from the preset-K=32 chunk-state buffers, ~+0.8 GB/shard on 1M-token batches; 100% GPU util while 10-50x slow). Fixed via get_result per-shard resume run SEQUENTIALLY then eval_sharded relaunch-skip-merge (run_lad_preset1_evalfix.cmd, commit 5338c49); sequential shards ~47 min per shard remainder, no thrash at 3.75 GB. RULE: elevated-VRAM rungs (K=32 streams) use sequential shards. Wall-clock: WS 2h33m, decay 38m, eval (wedged 2h + sequential 1h37m).

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER user rung 1 (iter 6): user stream n_heads 2->1 at fixed d_model=32 (RWKV_STREAM_HEADS=user:1) -- user per-entity WKV state 1728->3264 floats (1.89x, cap ~50x) at 198 fewer params; card/note/deck/preset unchanged; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs, sequential-shard eval
note: THE FIRST LADDER RUNG WITH A REAL SIGNAL. Paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2): ahead +0.000345 (clears >=0.0003) p=1.3e-20, imm +0.000258 p=1.5e-29 -- BOTH modes improve with overwhelming significance, but imm misses the magnitude bar by 0.000042 -> strict gate = REJECT. Contrast deck (iter 4) and preset (iter 5): p=1.0, no effect at all. So the state-sensitivity prior was RIGHT about which stream matters (user = longest recurrence; it also sank blanket state-quant) even though H=1 alone did not clear the gate. Validation during WS tracked the champion within noise the whole run (the val set is only 10 users -- too small to see a 0.0003 effect). Per research-conduct rule 2 (near-miss -> variant), NEXT = iter 7 lad_user2: user H=1 + user layers 3->4 (RWKV_STREAM_LAYERS=user:4) -- user state 3264->4352 floats (2.5x baseline), params ~204k <= 225k cap -- pushing the same knob harder. Wall-clock: WS 2h26m, decay 37m, sequential eval 2h24m (~5.5h total), no incidents (sequential-shard rule worked; shard 0 71 min, shard 1 71 min).

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER user rung 2 (iter 7): iter-6 recipe (user n_heads 2->1) + user layers 3->4 (RWKV_STREAM_LAYERS=user:4) -- user per-entity WKV state 3264->4352 floats (2.52x champion), params 193,526->203,928 (+10.4k = one d=32 layer); card/note/deck/preset unchanged; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs, sequential-shard eval
note: A MODE TRADE, not a win: paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2) ahead -0.000299 (p=1.0) / imm +0.000604 (p=7.8e-143). vs iter 6 (user H=1 alone): ahead -0.000643, imm +0.000346 -- the 4th user layer strongly boosts immediate-mode calibration but REGRESSES the ahead (cold forgetting-curve) mode. Combined attribution across the two rungs: state doubling (H=1, params ~flat) -> +ahead strongly +imm weakly; depth (+10.4k params + more state) -> +imm strongly -ahead. Neither variant clears BOTH bars. NEXT (iter 8): the lesson-bank SEED-PAIR DOCTRINE applies to iter 6's 0.000042 imm miss (thin-margin verdicts need the exact recipe re-run at a second RWKV_AUGMENT_SEED) -> lad_user1b = exact iter-6 recipe at seed 4321. If it clears both bars -> 1/2 seeds pass = Andrew's judgment call; same pattern -> reject stands (real but sub-bar; revisit if any later change adds imm); null -> iter 6 was partly seed luck. Wall-clock iter 7: WS 2h35m, decay 38m, sequential eval 2h26m (~5.7h), no incidents.

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER user rung 1 SEED-PAIR (iter 8): exact iter-6 recipe (user n_heads 2->1, RWKV_STREAM_HEADS=user:1) re-run at RWKV_AUGMENT_SEED=4321 -- lesson-bank seed-pair doctrine applied to iter 6's 0.000042 imm near-miss; params/state identical to iter 6; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs, sequential-shard eval; vprune ON vs seed-1234 champion trace with deltas widened to 0.006/0.008
status: REJECTED (NULL at seed 4321 -- iter 6 was seed luck)
note: THE SEED-PAIR KILLED IT: at seed 4321 the user-H=1 change shows NO improvement at all -- paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2) ahead -0.000044 (p=0.88) / imm -0.000146 (p=1.0), the same no-effect signature as deck (iter 4) and preset (iter 5). Iter 6's seed-1234 signal (+0.000345/+0.000258, p 1e-20/1e-29) does NOT replicate -> per the pre-declared outcome branches the reject STANDS and iter 6 reads as substantially seed luck (the augmentation-seed lottery can produce a 0.0003-class both-modes 'improvement' with astronomical in-seed p-values -- in-seed Wilcoxon p measures per-user consistency of the delta, NOT robustness across training randomness; the sibling's 64-b/56-b deaths showed the same failure mode). LADDER VERDICT: all three H=1 free-state rungs are now closed (deck null, preset null, user null-on-net across 2 seeds); user depth (iter 7) was a mode trade. The state-size ladder produced ZERO accepted rungs -- at d=32/H=2 no stream is state-capacity-limited enough for 2x recurrent memory to clear +0.0003 both modes. Cross-seed spread observed on the SAME recipe: ahead 0.306285 vs 0.306674 (0.00039), imm 0.277635 vs 0.278039 (0.00040) -- calibrates seed noise at the 0.0004 scale for single-run deltas near the gate; margins under ~0.0005 should default to seed-pair confirmation. Widened vprune (0.006/0.008) ran clean: no strikes, no false kills. NEXT: ladder CLOSED -> the >=50-iteration research phase begins (many idea families, arch + training; queued: warmup distillation from the d=128 teacher, data-driven init, cross-head readout mix, LIT_REVIEW). Wall-clock: WS 2h30m, decay 37m, sequential eval 2h23m, ~5.5h total, zero exceptions.

change: RESEARCH iter 9: shrink-perturb data-driven init (Ash & Adams 2020, queued idea scheme A) -- init = 0.5*champion_final(champ5kb1d_1638) + 0.5*fresh seeded draw (seed 777) via the new RWKV_INIT_BLEND hook (params only; deliberate zeros stay zero, decay ramps draw-invariant; smoke-verified). Otherwise EXACT champion recipe: champion arch H=2/K=16, champion HPs, seed 1234, 1-ep WS + 0.25-ep decay, q72u learnable cbs, parallel 2-shard eval; vprune ON standard deltas
note: Paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2): ahead -0.000744 (p=1.0) / imm -0.001033 (p=1.0). Both deltas EXCEED the ~0.0004 cross-seed spread calibrated by iter 8, so this is a genuine (small) harm, not seed lottery -- no seed-pair needed for a reject this clear. INSTRUCTIVE TRAJECTORY: the blended init led the champion val curve at every WS checkpoint (step 1000: -0.0104/-0.0093 val logloss; step 3500: only -0.0006/-0.0001; flat thereafter) -- the warm-start advantage washed out as both runs converged on the same data, and the inherited half-structure ended NET NEGATIVE after decay at full eval. Reading: at fixed 1-ep budget on the SAME 5000 users, lam=0.5 inheritance neither preserves the champion basin (fresh noise destroys it at full peak LR) nor explores freely (the inherited correlations bias early steps); both endpoints of the lam curve (~0=from-scratch champion, ~1=2-ep budget A/B) are champion-level and the midpoint sits BELOW -- the interior is a shallow dip, so the lambda probe {0.3,0.7} is NOT worth GPU. Data-driven-init family (scheme A shrink-perturb) CLOSED at this budget; scheme B (seeded permutation init) is a different mechanism, queued LOW. Ops: vprune never fired (candidate led val throughout -- as designed for a warm start); zero exceptions; wall-clock WS 2h32m + decay 38m + parallel eval 91m = ~4.7h. NEXT: iter 10 = warmup-only KD from the d=128 teacher (machinery committed 78caceb: stored-dump design, checksum-verified batch pairing, alpha 1->0 over 800 WS steps) -- dump smoke -> full 800-step dump -> student run.
status: REJECTED (worse both modes -- a real negative, beyond seed noise)

change: RESEARCH iter 10: warmup-only KD from the d=128 teacher (Andrew's queued idea) -- first 800 WS steps train on annealed mixed targets alpha*teacher + (1-alpha)*hard, alpha linear 1->0, from a STORED 800-step teacher dump (per-step labels-checksum pairing guard); hard labels thereafter. Everything else exact champ5k_b1 recipe (champion arch H=2/K=16, champion HPs, seed 1234, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs)
note: Paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2): ahead -0.000277 / imm -0.000329, p=1.0 both -- worse in BOTH modes, beyond the ~0.0004 cross-seed noise on imm and just at it on ahead = the KD warmup is neutral-to-harmful, no seed-pair needed for a reject. Trajectory mirrored iter 9 exactly: the teacher-mixed targets LED validation early (-0.0026/-0.0046 at step 500, still -0.0025/-0.0015 at 1500) then washed out to even by WS end and finished slightly negative at full eval. LESSON (now 2/2 in the family): early-training interventions that boost the first ~1000 steps (shrink-perturb init, teacher-KD warmup) do NOT survive 6554 steps of hard-label training on 5k users -- the optimizer forgets the head start; the EARLY-TRAINING-INTERVENTION family is now CLOSED (do not try curriculum-style warmups without a mechanism that persists past the window). OPS: the 2-parallel-shard eval WEDGED on the CHAMPION arch (both shards frozen 66+ min at 11.7/12 GB, 100% util -- two mega-users collided; the iter-5 elevated-VRAM-only scoping was too narrow). Killed + sequential-resume evalfix (run_iter10_kd_evalfix.cmd); RULE UPDATED: ALL evals now sequential shards. Wall-clock: dump 20 min (prior) + WS 2h28m + decay 38m + eval (wedged ~1.6h + sequential 1h37m). KD machinery (RWKV_KD_DUMP_OUT / RWKV_KD_MIX + checksum guard) stays in-repo for reuse.

change: RESEARCH iter 11: additive grade embedding (Andrew's idea) -- x = features2card(f) + grade_onehot @ E, E 4x32 ZERO-INIT bypass around the shared input MLP (a literal one-hot->embedding swap is a no-op: the first Linear already embeds the 4 grade cols; the bypass frees grade info from the fc->32 squeeze; matmul form keeps ahead-mode query rows at exactly zero). RWKV_GRADE_EMB=1 hook in srs_model.py, +128 params; else exact champ5k_b1 recipe
note: Paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2): ahead -0.000851 / imm -0.000908, p=1.0 both -- clearly worse, ~2x beyond the ~0.0004 cross-seed noise; a real harm, no seed-pair needed. NOT a near-miss (conduct rule 2 does not force a variant). Val trace looked champion-level all run (10-user val can't resolve 0.001) -- the harm only showed at full eval. Interpretation: the unregularized linear bypass injects the grade one-hot straight into the trunk that ALL 5 streams share, bypassing the MLP's SiLU/LayerNorm; plausibly distorts the shared representation more than it helps the grade signal (grade info was never bottlenecked -- 4 of 92 dims through a 128-wide fc is plenty). GRADE-REPRESENTATION family 0/1, deprioritized (rule 5: not closed); untried variants if revisited: per-stream additive embeddings after each stream input (+640 params), grade embedding into the SRS heads instead of the trunk, LayerNorm on the bypass. Wall-clock: WS 2h33m, decay 38m, sequential eval 2h25m (~5.6h), no incidents (first run under the new all-sequential-eval rule). Hook stays in-repo (env-gated, default off = byte-identical).

change: RESEARCH iter 12: SRS-head resolution 64->128 (RWKV_NUM_CURVES=128 + RWKV_NUM_POINTS=128) -- capacity re-test at 5k data of the 100u exp1 reject (that lesson was data-limitation-scoped). Pure params (+16.5k, 210,236 total), ZERO state cost, Rust auto-derives head dims; else exact champ5k_b1 recipe
status: REJECTED (no effect: both modes ~-0.00025, within seed noise)
note: Paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2): ahead -0.000270 / imm -0.000241, p=1.0 both -- the deck/preset no-effect signature (magnitude inside the ~0.0004 cross-seed band). The 100u 'capacity adds fail' lesson does NOT flip at 5k for this lever: 64 basis curves / 64 sample points are simply enough resolution for the forgetting-curve mixture; doubling them adds params without signal. Val trace sat at champion parity the whole run (WS-end +0.0003/+0.0010), fully consistent with the null verdict. CAPACITY-AT-5K family 0/1 so far (channel mixer 1.0->1.5 is the next in-family variant, queued as iter 13). Wall-clock: WS 2h32m, decay 38m, sequential eval 2h24m (~5.6h), no incidents -- second clean run under the all-sequential-eval rule.

change: RESEARCH iter 13: channel mixer factor 1.0 -> 1.5 (RWKV_CHANNEL_MIXER_FACTOR=1.5, per-block FFN width mult) -- capacity re-test at 5k data, in-family variant after iter 12's SRS-head-resolution null. Pure params (+14.3k, 208,060 total), ZERO state cost; else exact champ5k_b1 recipe
status: REJECTED (no effect: both modes ~-0.0002, within seed noise)
note: Paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2): ahead -0.000159 (p=0.999) / imm -0.000271 (p=1.0) -- the no-effect signature (inside the ~0.0004 cross-seed band). CAPACITY-AT-5K family 0/2 (SRS-head resolution 64->128, channel mixer 1.5): the d=32 trunk is NOT capacity-limited at 5k users in either the heads or the FFN width -- the d=128 gap (+0.0102/+0.0134) lives elsewhere (likely recurrent/width capacity of the streams themselves, which state-ladder H=1 also failed to buy). Val trace led mid-WS (up to -0.0026 ahead at step 4500) and washed out by WS end -- another washout instance. Clean ~5.6h run, no incidents. LAST QAT-ERA ITERATION: methodology switches to plain-vs-plain screening after this (Andrew 2026-07-14) -- QAT parked until the end of research, one quant-aware run of the final champion at close.
